subject: Accounting
title: Adjusting for Prepaid Expenses
description: One-minute skill: Adjusting for Prepaid Expenses.

hook.text: You pay $12,000 for a full year of insurance in January. Is the entire amount a January expense?

buildup.text: Prepaid expenses are costs paid in advance for future benefit—insurance, rent, subscriptions.

discovery.text: Each month, $1,000 moves from prepaid insurance (asset) to insurance expense—matching the period.

twist.text: Without this adjustment, January looks expensive and the next 11 months look free—distorting profits.

climax.text: Adjusting entries ensure each period bears only the costs it actually consumed.

punchline.text: Pay once, expense monthly. Matching demands it.

quiz.question: How are prepaid expenses adjusted?
options: By moving the used portion from asset to expense each period
By recording the full amount immediately
By ignoring them until year end
